breaking_news.title: Iranian APT MuddyWater Backdoors US Critical Infrastructure
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: A Iran-linked APT group, MuddyWater (affiliated with MOIS), has been embedded in multiple U.S. networks—including a bank, software firm, and airport—since early February, with increased activity following U.S.-Israeli military strikes on Iran. Researchers from ClearSky and other firms confirmed the compromise using custom implants and persistent access mechanisms.

Paragraph 2: The intrusion targets high-value national infrastructure sectors, enabling long-term espionage and potential disruption. The compromise of a U.S. bank and airport signals possible readiness for kinetic-cyber operations, raising concerns about supply chain and lateral movement risks across critical sectors.

breaking_news.title: Cisco SD-WAN Flaws Actively Exploited in Wild
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: Cisco confirmed active exploitation of CVE-2026-20122 and CVE-2026-20128 in Catalyst SD-WAN Manager, allowing authenticated remote attackers to overwrite arbitrary files and potentially achieve remote code execution. These flaws affect enterprise SD-WAN deployments globally and have been weaponized in real-world attacks.

Paragraph 2: As SD-WAN appliances sit at network perimeters and manage critical traffic routing, compromise enables broad lateral movement, data interception, and infrastructure disruption. The active exploitation status and high CVSS scores (7.1+) make this a top priority for patching across Fortune 500 and government networks.

breaking_news.title: Coruna iOS Exploit Kit Used in Mass Crypto Theft Campaigns
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: Google’s GTIG identified the Coruna iOS exploit kit—a sophisticated set of 23 exploits across five chains targeting iOS 13–17.2.1—now being used in financially motivated campaigns, including crypto theft. Originally developed for state-sponsored actors, the kit has proliferated to criminal groups, indicating a thriving zero-day marketplace.

Paragraph 2: The kit’s reuse by multiple threat actors—including Russian espionage and Chinese cybercriminals—demonstrates how high-end exploits leak and get repurposed. With over 200 million iOS devices potentially vulnerable, this poses a systemic risk to mobile security and digital asset protection globally.

breaking_news.title: LeakBase Cybercrime Forum Seized in Global Takedown
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: Europol and FBI-led Operation Leak dismantled LeakBase, a major cybercrime forum active since 2021 with over 142,000 users, used to trade stolen credentials, stealer logs, and zero-day exploits. The takedown involved 14 countries and seized server infrastructure in Amsterdam.

Paragraph 2: LeakBase served as a central hub for the cybercrime ecosystem—facilitating access brokering, credential resale, and tool exchange. Its disruption significantly weakens the underground economy supporting ransomware, phishing, and APT operations, marking one of the most impactful law enforcement actions against cybercrime infrastructure in recent years.

breaking_news.title: Tycoon 2FA Phishing Platform Dismantled Globally
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: Europol, Microsoft, and partners dismantled Tycoon 2FA, a leading phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) platform used to bypass MFA via adversary-in-the-middle (AitM) attacks. Microsoft seized 330 domains powering the service, while law enforcement in six European countries executed raids.

Paragraph 2: Tycoon 2FA accounted for ~62% of all phishing attacks targeting MFA as of mid-2025 and was used in attacks on hundreds of thousands of organizations, including hospitals and schools. Its takedown delivers a major blow to credential theft campaigns and restores trust in MFA as a protective control.

breaking_news.title: APT28 Deploys New BadPaw Loader and MeowMeow Backdoor in Ukraine
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: Russian APT28 launched a new campaign targeting Ukrainian entities using two previously undocumented malware families: BadPaw (a .NET loader) and MeowMeow (a sophisticated backdoor). The attack chain begins with phishing emails containing ZIP archives and HTA files displaying Ukrainian-language lures about border crossing appeals.

Paragraph 2: This campaign reflects APT28’s continued focus on Ukraine amid ongoing conflict, with potential for intelligence gathering and disruption of critical government and defense systems. The use of novel malware underscores APT28’s evolving tradecraft and ability to deploy custom tooling rapidly.

breaking_news.title: Cisco Secure FMC Critical Flaws (CVSS 10.0) Patched
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: Cisco patched two maximum-severity vulnerabilities in its Secure Firewall Management Center (FMC): CVE-2026-20079 (authentication bypass) and CVE-2026-20131 (insecure deserialization), both rated CVSS 10.0. These flaws allow unauthenticated remote attackers to gain root access to managed firewalls via the web interface.

Paragraph 2: Though not yet reported as actively exploited, the flaws are trivial to exploit once reverse-engineered and could enable large-scale firewall compromise, network reconfiguration, or traffic interception. Organizations using Cisco FMC must prioritize patching immediately to prevent catastrophic infrastructure compromise.

breaking_news.title: Google Reports 90 Zero-Days Exploited in 2025
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: Google’s Threat Intelligence Group tracked 90 zero-day vulnerabilities actively exploited in 2025—up from 78 in 2024—nearly half impacting enterprise software and appliances. The report highlights increased exploitation by commercial spyware vendors and state-sponsored actors, with China and spyware vendors leading attribution.

Paragraph 2: This sustained high volume signals a normalization of zero-day operations across threat actors. The rise in enterprise-targeted zero-days reflects a strategic shift from consumer-focused attacks, demanding urgent investment in detection, response, and proactive threat hunting capabilities.
